text.title: risk-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for risk-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stale risk register never reviewed after creation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 6-month-old register is worse than none because it creates false confidence. Risks have resolved, new risks have emerged, and the register now misleads rather than informs. Weekly 15-minute reviews are mandatory.

failure-mode.description: Using "Accept" response without a contingency budget line.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Accept" is the most-abused response: it lets teams skip planning while pretending to manage the risk. Accepting without provisioning a reserve is hoping. Every Accept must map to a specific contingency amount equal to its EMV.

failure-mode.description: Double-tracking materialized risks as both a Risk and an Issue.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When a risk materializes, convert it to an Issue and remove it from the register. Keeping it in both places inflates the risk count and confuses status reporting.

failure-mode.description: Setting risk reviews as 2-hour quarterly events instead of 15-minute weekly sessions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Quarterly reviews find risks that have already become crises. Weekly 15-minute reviews catch drift while corrective action is still affordable.
